name: FIFARMA WAIT indicator Latin America 2024 - quantitative access data
country: LATAM (Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Mexico, Panama, Peru, Dominican Republic)
access_mechanism_type: Cross-cutting analysis of formal_procurement and pooled_regional access mechanisms. Measures time from FDA/EMA approval to publicly available, limited or fully available status across LATAM health systems. Methodology developed by IQVIA for FIFARMA modeled on European EFPIA Patient W.A.I.T. indicator.
procurement_channels: Indicator measures availability across all formal channels (national health systems, social security insurers, public formularies). Includes both regulatory approval and inclusion in public coverage as gating events.
key_actors: FIFARMA (Federacion Latinoamericana de la Industria Farmaceutica) - sponsor and publisher. IQVIA - methodology partner and data provider. Local pharmaceutical industry associations (CAEME-Argentina, Interfarma-Brazil, CIF-Chile, AFIDRO-Colombia, FEDEFARMA-Costa Rica, AMIIF-Mexico, ALAFARPE-Peru, ASIFAN-Panama, INFADOMI-Dominican Republic, ASEDIM-Ecuador). National regulatory authorities being measured: ANMAT (Argentina), ANVISA (Brazil), ISP (Chile), INVIMA (Colombia), CECMED, COFEPRIS (Mexico), DIGEMID (Peru), ARCSA (Ecuador). ISPOR conferences for academic dissemination.
formulary_coverage: Methodology distinguishes (a) regulatory approval, (b) reimbursement/public listing, (c) full vs partial public availability. 61% of internationally approved medicines (FDA or EMA) have local regulatory approval in at least one of the 10 LATAM countries; only 35% have full or partial public availability. For medicines approved internationally as of 2020, less than 12% have full or partial public availability across the region (extreme lag).
judicial_access_volume: Not measured by WAIT but related: judicial channels (tutela in Colombia, amparo in Argentina) often the only effective access route for the 89% of recently approved drugs not yet in formal coverage. [uncertain: WAIT specific tutela/amparo cross-reference]
registration_timeline_generic: Not the focus of WAIT (which measures innovative drugs). [uncertain]
registration_timeline_biologic: Not specifically broken out in WAIT 2024 summary. [uncertain]
biosimilar_pathway: Not specifically measured by WAIT (focuses on originator innovative drugs). [uncertain]
registration_backlog: Indirectly measured through delay metrics. Country-specific backlog not summarized in WAIT 2024 results extract. [uncertain: country-by-country breakdown]
reform_status: Not applicable - WAIT is a measurement indicator, not a reform.
currency_mechanism: Not directly measured but currency volatility indirectly affects formulary inclusion decisions.
financial_mechanism: Indirectly: WAIT distinguishes 'limited public availability' (selective reimbursement) from 'full public availability' (broad coverage). Financial_mechanism diversity across countries explains delay heterogeneity.
payer_debt_crisis: Not measured but cited as factor by FIFARMA in commentary.
shortage_rate: Not directly measured (WAIT measures access delay, not shortage). However, 87% no-progress rate for orphan/oncology in 2023-2024 implies de facto access shortage for these therapeutic areas.
shortage_causes: WAIT identifies systemic causes: (1) regulatory approval delays after FDA/EMA; (2) Health Technology Assessment (HTA) processes adding additional months/years; (3) reimbursement listing delays; (4) budget impact assessments; (5) negotiation cycles. Country-specific differences relate to local HTA institutional maturity and budget constraints.
shortage_monitoring_system: WAIT itself is a monitoring system - third edition published October 2024.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: Oncology one of five therapeutic areas measured. Of treatments for orphan and oncological diseases, 87% had no progress in regulatory approval or public access in the last year (2023-2024) per FIFARMA WAIT 2024 - the most acutely affected therapeutic area along with rare diseases.
source_citation: 1) IQVIA & FIFARMA, 'White Paper FIFARMA Patient W.A.I.T. Indicator 2024 - Latin America' (October 2024), https://fifarma.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/IQVIA-FIFARMA-WAIT-2024-White-Paper.pdf. 2) FIFARMA, 'Los pacientes latinoamericanos deben esperar en promedio 4.7 anos para acceder a tratamientos innovadores', https://fifarma.org/en/waitindicator2024/. 3) GaBI Online, 'Latin American patients face 4.7-year wait for innovative treatments', https://gabionline.net/reports/latin-american-patients-face-4.7-year-wait-for-innovative-treatments. 4) FIFARMA W.A.I.T. Indicator 2024 event page, https://fifarma.org/en/events/fifarma-w-a-i-t-indicator-2024/. 5) ISPOR, 'Patient WAIT Survey Findings Across 8 LATAM Countries' (Feb 14, 2024 webinar). 6) FIFARMA Patient W.A.I.T. Indicator 2024 - Latin America (ENG), https://fifarma.org/en/publications/fifarma-patient-w-a-i-t-indicator-2024-latin-america/.
data_reliability: medium - methodologically rigorous (modeled on EFPIA WAIT) but industry-association-funded; should be triangulated with PAHO/government data. Sample of 365 molecules represents 80% of new active substances launched 2014-2023.
kb_action: new_doc_needed - feeds new_latam_access.txt with quantitative access data (4.75y mean delay, 87% no-progress orphan/oncology, 35% full/partial public availability, 12% public availability of 2020-approved drugs across 10 countries)
